pending.label: Edda - Letter 1 variant A (a seat that is not mine)
pending.variant:
My name is Edda Marsh.

I am writing this from a seat that is not mine.

My seat was three carriages behind. I left it quickly. Not in fear. Closer to the way you leave a room when the kettle has been on too long.

I will explain when I have walked further. For now: I have moved. I have begun moving.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 1 variant B (the unfolded shawl)
pending.variant:
Edda Marsh.

This seat is warm. Not from me.

My seat is back the way I came. Three carriages, perhaps four. I did not count.

My shawl is still on it. Folded. Folding was the last thing I did not do.

More when I have walked further.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 2 variant A (three heads at the old window)
pending.variant:
I am in a sleeping car. The bed has been made. Not by me.

Last night something was at my old window.

Three heads. Bone the colour of wet ash. The middle larger than the others.

They moved one at a time. The middle would still and the two beside it would turn. All three looked in.

I did not look away. There seemed no reason to. After a while they were gone.

I waited an hour. Then I got up. Then I walked.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 2 variant B (I have looked at worse)
pending.variant:
A sleeping car. The bed is made. Not by me.

Three heads at the old window last night.

Bone-grey. Middle one larger. The two beside it turned when it did not.

They all looked in.

I have looked at worse things in my life. I looked back.

After a while they were gone. I waited. I walked.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 3 variant A (the library car experiment)
pending.variant:
I walked forward four carriages. I am writing from a reading nook in what may be a library car. Some of the books are real. Some are spine-only and glued to the wall.

It is here too. At my window. Not the one I left. This new one.

The same three heads. The middle one tilted slightly off centre, as if listening.

I walked to the other side of the car. That window was dark. I came back. It was at mine again.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 3 variant B (bright with bone)
pending.variant:
Four carriages forward. A library car. Some of the books are real. Some are spines glued to the wall.

It is here too. At this window. Not the one I left.

Same three heads. Middle one tilted, as if listening.

I crossed the car. The other window was dark. I came back. Mine was bright with bone again.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 4 variant A (Hollin, soul sand, what came up)
pending.variant:
I am in a goods car. The crates are nailed shut. The light is the only thing not boxed.

I should tell you about Hollin.

He was my husband. He dug soul sand. He sold most of it. He kept a little for himself.

He dug too deep, in the end. Something came up out of the place he was digging.

Three heads. A middle one larger than the others. A tilt to the middle one. As if listening.

Hollin did not come up.

I have been waiting a long time to see that face again. Now I have.

It is at every window I sit by.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 4 variant B (Hollin stayed)
pending.variant:
A goods car. Crates nailed shut. The light is the only thing not boxed.

Hollin.

He was my husband. He dug soul sand. Sold most. Kept a little.

He dug too deep. Something came up.

Three heads. Middle one larger. Tilted. As if listening.

Hollin stayed.

I waited a long time to see that face again.

Now I have.

Edda

pending.label: Edda - Letter 5 variant A (the windows show what we carry)
pending.variant:
I am in an observation car. Long windows. Many of them. A few other passengers.

I sat here for hours. I watched the windows. Not out of them. At them.

The train moves at a steady pace. The view does not. A tree stays beside us for too long. A river runs the wrong way and then runs ours.

The next carriage had its own view. A different valley. The carriage after that had a field I knew when I was twelve.

There is a man two seats down who is watching a kitchen. I think it is his mother's kitchen. He has been crying very quietly for an hour.

The windows do not show outside. They show what the watcher carries.

I have been carrying Hollin. I have been carrying what came up out of his digging.

It is not following me. It has always been mine.

I am steady. I want you to know I am steady.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 5 variant B (not following, mine)
pending.variant:
Observation car. Long windows. Several. A few other passengers.

I watched the windows. Not out of them. At them. For hours.

The train moves at a steady pace. The view does not. A tree stays too long. A river runs the wrong way and then runs ours.

Next carriage: a different valley. The one after: a field I knew when I was twelve.

Two seats down a man is watching a kitchen. I think it is his mother's. He has been crying very quietly.

The windows show what we carry.

I have been carrying Hollin. And what came up out of his digging.

It has always been mine. Not following. Mine.

I am steady.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 6 variant A (lifted the latch, for whoever finds the seat next)
pending.variant:
I have stopped walking.

I am in a small carriage near the back. Or near the front. The light is the same in both directions.

There is a window. There is a latch on the window. I have been looking at the latch for a long while.

The three heads are here. They are not at the glass now. They are a little way out from it, in the dark. Waiting.

I have decided I am not going to walk again. I have decided I am going to lift the latch.

I have lifted the latch.

The window is open. The night is very cold. The cedar smells stronger when the air moves through it.

I am going to leave this here. I am going to leave the window open too. For whoever finds the seat next.

pending.label: Edda - Letter 6 variant B (leaving the window open too)
pending.variant:
I have stopped.

A small carriage. Near the back. Or the front. The light is the same in both directions.

A window. A latch. I have been looking at it a long while.

The three heads are here. Not at the glass now. A little way out. Waiting.

I am not going to walk again. I am going to lift the latch.

I have lifted it.

The window is open. The night is cold. The cedar smells stronger when the air moves.

Leaving this here.

Leaving the window open too.
